title: Congress Temporarily Suspends ‘Nasha Mukta Odisha Jan Jagaran Abhiyan’ Amid Heatwave - Hindusthan Samachar English
full_text:
Bhubaneswar, 07 May (H.S.) : The Odisha Pradesh Congress Committee has temporarily suspended its ongoing “Nasha Mukta Odisha Jan Jagaran Abhiyan” in view of the intense heatwave conditions prevailing across western Odisha.
The announcement was made by Odisha Pradesh Congress Scheduled Caste Department President and advocate Manas Kumar Mallik during a press conference held at Congress Bhavan on Thursday.
Mallik said the awareness campaign was launched on April 14 from Suando in Puri district under the guidance of Odisha Pradesh Congress Committee President Bhakta Charan Das, with the objective of creating awareness against substance abuse and promoting a drug-free Odisha.
As part of the campaign, Congress workers travelled across several districts, including Puri, Jagatsinghpur, Kendrapara, Jajpur, Bhadrak, Balasore, Mayurbhanj, Keonjhar, Dhenkanal, Angul, Deogarh, Sundargarh, Jharsuguda, and Sambalpur.
During the outreach programme, roadside public meetings and awareness drives were conducted at crowded locations to educate people about the harmful effects of addiction.
Mallik stated that the campaign has been put on temporary hold following the advice of PCC President Bhakta Charan Das due to severe heat and hot wind conditions in western Odisha. He added that the remaining districts would be covered once weather conditions improve.
He further noted that the 22-day-long awareness campaign began from the sacred land of Lord Jagannath and has presently taken a temporary pause at Sambalpur, the abode of Maa Samaleswari.

Hindusthan Samachar / Monalisa Panda
